introSection: Dania Beach Hurricane was a wooden roller coaster previously located at Boomers in Dania Beach, Florida, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Park founder Jules Ross first planned to add a roller coaster in 1996. When he sold the park in 1996, he leased three acres of land adjacent to the park to the new owner, Festival Fun Parks, to allow the coaster to be built. Ross intended for Dania Beach Hurricane to be the first in a series of five wooden roller coasters, but the other attractions never came to fruition.

The roller coaster's construction triggered a series of lawsuits, including from McKinley Steel and Supply, Anchor Petroleum, Professional Foundations of Pompano Beach, and C&C Concrete Pumping Inc. Ross faulted manufacturer Coaster Works, Inc. for the issues and said that the company could not finish the construction project within its budget and had to come up with an additional $350,000 to finish the project.

The ride opened on November 1, 2000. Seats on the first official ride were auctioned to benefit United Cerebral Palsy.

The ride operated from 2000 to 2011. It was demolished in 2016.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The layout was modeled after the Predator at Six Flags Darien Lake.

fullText:
Dania Beach Hurricane was a wooden roller coaster previously located at Boomers in Dania Beach, Florida, USA.

Park founder Jules Ross first planned to add a roller coaster in 1996. When he sold the park in 1996, he leased three acres of land adjacent to the park to the new owner, Festival Fun Parks, to allow the coaster to be built. Ross intended for Dania Beach Hurricane to be the first in a series of five wooden roller coasters, but the other attractions never came to fruition.

The roller coaster's construction triggered a series of lawsuits, including from McKinley Steel and Supply, Anchor Petroleum, Professional Foundations of Pompano Beach, and C&C Concrete Pumping Inc. Ross faulted manufacturer Coaster Works, Inc. for the issues and said that the company could not finish the construction project within its budget and had to come up with an additional $350,000 to finish the project.

The ride opened on November 1, 2000. Seats on the first official ride were auctioned to benefit United Cerebral Palsy.

The ride operated from 2000 to 2011. It was demolished in 2016.

The layout was modeled after the Predator at Six Flags Darien Lake.
